family_summary: Interstate and civil war on and around the Korean Peninsula involving North Korea, South Korea, the United States-led UN Command, and later the PRC. For DR work, a single whole-war denominator is misleading because fighting, occupation, and displacement patterns changed drastically across cities, corridors, and fronts.
notes: This bundle follows an episode-first strategy focused on front/city episodes with displacement-aware civilian denominators. It is not an exhaustive partition of all 1950-1953 combat sectors.
